overview: Izzet Prowess is a tempo strategy that leverages low-cost creatures with the Prowess mechanic to apply rapid pressure while cycling through the deck. By repeatedly casting instants and sorceries, the deck triggers Prowess to make creatures larger and faster, overwhelming opponents before they can stabilize. The package relies on efficient spell generation and evasion to bypass traditional removal.
win_conditions: Trading up with growing Prowess creatures
Overwhelming the opponent with multiple evasive threats
Casting a large number of instants to trigger Prowess repeatedly

keep_criteria: Hands with one-mana creatures and cheap instants
Draw engines that generate card advantage
Hands with at least three spells to trigger Prowess
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing duals or mana rocks), at least one cheap cantrip or mana spell (e.g., Arcum's Astrolabe, Manamorphose), and a Prowess enabler or early damage spell.
Keep any hand with a turn-1 playable spell or mana accelerator.
mulligan_criteria: Hands missing instant or sorcery spells
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no spells, or 0-1 lands with no cantrips/rituals to fix mana.

early_game: Play one-drop creatures to establish board presence
Cast cheap instants to trigger Prowess and cycle
Apply early pressure to force opponent responses
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or mana spell to trigger Prowess and dig.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast a second spell to trigger Prowess again, aiming to draw into threats or burn.
Prioritize playing lands that enter untapped or can produce multiple colors, and sequence spells to maximize Prowess triggers before committing to combat.
mid_game: Use card draw to maintain spell density
Protect key threats with counterspells or evasion
Trade efficiently while growing creatures larger
prioritize casting multiple spells per turn to trigger Prowess and dig deeper.
Manage mana carefully to ensure you can cast 2+ spells on key turns.
Use graveyard strategically if the deck utilizes it, but avoid overcommitting to graveyard synergies unless necessary.
Hold back on committing all threats if the opponent has removal or counterspells.
Use Prowess triggers to scout and stabilize before going wide.
late_game: Flood the board with multiple Prowess creatures
Use remaining spells to trigger Prowess for lethal damage
Stabilize against opponent finishers with removal or counters
Direct damage spells (Lightning Strike, Grapeshot, Aetherflux Reservoir) or combat damage from Prowess-enriched threats.
Win by reducing opponent to 0 life through spell chains or combat.
Go all-in when you have enough Prowess triggers to draw into your finisher, or when the opponent is at lethal range (10 or less life).
Commit when you can safely cast 3+ spells in a turn to ensure you draw through your deck.

key_cards.name: Astrologian's Planisphere
key_cards.notes: Cast instants to draw cards and fuel Prowess triggers

key_cards.name: Stormchaser's Talent
key_cards.notes: Use cheap spells to trigger Prowess repeatedly while cycling

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast to draw cards and maintain a steady stream of instants for Prowess

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting instants before combat to maximize Prowess triggers
Hold up mana for counterspells when facing control decks
Cycle dead cards to find more instants and keep Prowess active
Common mistake: Overcommitting to combat without triggering Prowess first.
Always cast spells before attacking to maximize Prowess triggers.
ensure your deck has enough mana acceleration and dual lands.
Unique interaction: Prowess triggers on the stack, so you can cast multiple spells in response to each other to trigger Prowess repeatedly.
Signature cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Flow State are turn-2/3 finishers that also trigger Prowess, so sequence them after cheaper enablers to maximize card draw and mana generation.

matchups.advice: Use evasion to bypass early pressure and trade efficiently
Play around removal by keeping spells in hand to trigger Prowess
Race before they stabilize with value engines

Use Prowess to dig through counterspells, cast spells in response to triggers, or use cheap cantrips to find answers. Hold up mana for Prowess triggers to maintain tempo. Consider sideboarded artifact hate or counterspell disruption.
Apply early pressure to punish their mana development. Use direct damage to remove their ramp sources or finish them before they stabilize. Track their land drops and prepare burn.

Win by having better spell sequencing, more efficient mana, and superior sideboard choices. Prioritize disrupting their spell chains with counterspell hate and direct damage. Track their hand size and Prowess triggers to out-draw them.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (hold up mana for Prowess triggers or have backup plans), sweepers (prepare with artifact destruction or burn), and ramp threats (track mana development and prepare early pressure).
Key tell: Opponent holding 2+ mana on their turn often signals a counter or removal.
